records.ety: [L. illaqueatus, p. p. of illaqueare; pref. il- in + laqueare to insnare, fr. laqueus, noose, snare.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Illaqueated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Illaqueating.]

defs: To insnare; to entrap; to entangle; to catch.
quotes: Let not the surpassing eloquence of Taylor dazzle you, nor his scholastic retiary versatility of logic illaqueate your good sense.
